entry.title: Data Proposal for Sales Order Requests - Determination with Reference to Order Creation History
entry.content:
In the Create Sales Orders - Automatic Extraction app, the system extracts data (such as the delivery address and product description) from your uploaded file. Usually, such data can't be directly used for sales order creation, because sales order data (such as the sold-to party, ship-to party, and product IDs in your system) do not exist in the original purchase order file. The system needs to match the extracted data to the various IDs in your system, either through master data determination or determination with reference to order creation history.

This blog describes how the system determines the sold-to party, ship-to party, and product with reference to order creation history.

Why Determination with Reference to Order Creation History

Traditional master data determination relies heavily on extracted data for a determination. Consider the diversity of purchase order files, where the text format and layout may vary with countries, industries, customers, and even personal habits. The data extraction service may misidentify or fail to identify certain texts. Also, it may be difficult to find exact matches in your system when the extracted data differ much from the data maintained in your system. When master data determination fails, users need to check the purchase order file and specify sales order request fields. Such manual efforts typically recur for similar purchase order files.

Determination with reference to order creation history is designed to enable the system to learn from the repeated user preferences. By anticipating the same field values in similar contexts, this determination strategy effectively supplements traditional master data determination and would reduce manual efforts.

How Determination with Reference to Order Creation History Works

1.Sold-to party and Ship-to party

When the sold-to party address can be extracted for a new sales order request, the system considers the latest three sales order requests that have the same extracted sold-to party address and company code as the new sales order request. If the three sales order requests have the same sold-to party (for example, sold-to party A) and are completed (that is, order created), the system proposes the sold-to party (for example, as sold-to party A) for the new sales order request.

The processing logic for the ship-to party is the same.

2.Product

When product data (including the extracted product, extracted product description, and extracted customer material) can be extracted for a new sales order request item, the system considers the latest three sales order request items that have the same extracted product data and company code as the new sales order request item. If the three sales order request items have the same product (for example, product A), and their corresponding sales order requests are completed (that is, order created). the system proposes the product (for example, as product A) for the new sales order request item.

Notes:

  • When the status of a sales order request is completed, the sales order request has been converted to a sales order. The system treats such sales order requests as valid historical data.
  • For the sold-to/ship-to party, valid historical data must have the same extracted address. The extracted address consists of an extracted name and an extracted full address. The extracted name (marked in red boxes) shows in the extracted address data area in the header data of the sales order request.

    To view the extracted full address, you can choose the View File button in the app and find the information via extraction labels (marked in blue boxes).

    For the product, valid historical data must have the same extracted product data (including the extracted product, extracted product description, and extracted customer material).

  • The determination works only when at least three valid historical records exist.

Let’s see some examples.

(In each example, the company codes of the sales order requests are the same.)

Example 1

Your system contains historical records as follows. When you upload a file that has the same extracted sold-to party address (consisting of the name “MNN Ltd” and the full address “No.19 Street AAA 30019, BBB City, USA”), does the determination work?

Sales Order Request

Status

Sales Order

Extracted Sold-to Party Name

Extracted Sold-to Party Full address

Sold-Party

Created on

200021

Completed

30089

MNN Ltd

No.19 Street AAA 30019, BBB City, USA

10096

2024/01/20

200020

Completed

30087

MNN Ltd

No.19 Street AAA 30019, BBB City, USA

10085

2024/01/19

200018

Completed

30082

MNN Ltd

No.19 Street AAA 30019, BBB City, USA

10085

2024/01/18

200015

Completed

30077

MNN Ltd

No.19 Street AAA 30019, BBB City, USA

10085

2024/01/17

No. The system considers the latest three records but they do not have the same sold-to party.

Sales Order Request

Status

Sales Order

Extracted Sold-to Party Name

Extracted Sold-to Party Full address

Sold-Party

Created on

200021

Completed

30089

MNN Ltd

No.19 Street AAA 30019, BBB City, USA

10096

2024/01/20

200020

Completed

30087

MNN Ltd

No.19 Street AAA 30019, BBB City, USA

10085

2024/01/19

200018

Completed

30082

MNN Ltd

No.19 Street AAA 30019, BBB City, USA

10085

2024/01/18

Example 2

Your system contains historical records as follows. When you upload a file that has the same extracted sold-to party address (consisting of the name “MNN Ltd” and the full address “No.19 Street AAA 30019, BBB City, USA”), does the determination work?

Sales Order Request

Status

Sales Order

Extracted Sold-to Party Name

Extracted Sold-to Party Full address

Sold-Party

Created on

200046

In Process

 

MNN Ltd

No.19 Street AAA 30019, BBB City, USA

10085

2024/01/20

200043

Completed

30087

MNN Ltd

No.19 Street AAA 30019, BBB City, USA

10085

2024/01/19

200040

Completed

30082

MNN Ltd

No.19 Street AAA 30019, BBB City, USA

10085

2024/01/18

No. The system considers the latest three records but only two valid records exist (sales order request 200046 is not completed).

Example 3

Your system contains historical records as follows. When you upload a file that seems to have the same extracted sold-to party address, does the determination work?

Sales Order Request

Status

Sales Order

Extracted Sold-to Party Name

Extracted Sold-to Party Full address

Sold-Party

Created on

200021

Completed

30089

MNN LTD

No.19 Street AAA 30019, BBB City,

10085

2024/01/20

200020

Completed

30087

MNN Ltd

No.19, Street AAA 30019, BBB City, USA

10085

2024/01/19

200018

Completed

30082

MNN Ltd.

No.19 Street AAA 30019, BBB City, USA

10085

2024/01/18

200015

Completed

30077

MNN Ltd

No.19 Street AAA 30019, BBB City, USA

10085

2024/01/17

No. The extracted sold-to party address is not exactly the same for these sales order requests. The extracted name and full address are case-sensitive and should contain the same texts, including special characters (for example, comma).

Sales Order Request

Status

Sales Order

Extracted Sold-to Party Name

Extracted Sold-to Party Full address

Sold-Party

Created on

200021

Completed

30089

MNN LTD

No.19 Street AAA 30019, BBB City, ___

10085

2024/01/20

200020

Completed

30087

MNN Ltd

No.19, Street AAA 30019, BBB City, USA

10085

2024/01/19

200018

Completed

30082

MNN Ltd.

No.19 Street AAA 30019, BBB City, USA

10085

2024/01/18

200015

Completed

30077

MNN Ltd

No.19 Street AAA 30019, BBB City, USA

10085

2024/01/17

Summary

For the sold-to/ship-to party and product, determination with reference to order creation history (supposed to give the most accurate results) happens before determination from master data. As historical records grow, the chance of such determination should be higher. To check how these fields are determined, you can check the application log of the sales order request.

Related info

You can find series of blogs about this solution in below link.
The Link Collection for Create Sales Orders – Automatic Extraction | SAP Blogs

entry.title: No authorization to create tasklist for plant (general maintenance)
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I am facing this issue in the Create Task List app.

When the user is going to create task list, receive the error message: "No authorization to create tasklist for plant XXXX"

I´ve check Maintain Workforce and Maintain Business User apps and they seem to be ok.

Any ideas?

Thanks so much in advanced.

entry.title: Issue of new PB
entry.content:
Hi,
What is the licensing cost of the current Power Builder version? How to ensure the app (by it) is fine on the Android OS or iOs?

entry.title: Error Please Check ROUTE and SLOC
entry.content:
Hi Gurus,

In need of your guidance. I received the following error message from user:

As a workaround, I changed the Storage Location Address (previously SERANG) to CILEGON through OX09:

When changed, user was then able to use the Route.

 

Does Search Term & City in SLOC has a connection with Route?

Is Dep. Point in 0VTC is the same as City in OX09?

How can I keep the SLOC city as SERANG? and do I need to define new routes with SERANG as its Dep. Point?

 

Thank you for your help.

Warm regards,

Erika

entry.title: Meet PLM experts from SAP at Hannover Messe 2024
entry.content:
Make market-driven decisions for product innovation to drive growth and sustainability with AI

Companies are constantly searching for ways to improve their productivity and streamline their product innovations lead time from inception to disposal. The use of visualization tools is helping teams better collaborate and accelerate product development cycle times. In sync data from product development combined with CAD models support all downstream manufacturing processes to gain efficiency and transparency. This means a Digital Thread is the integration of product design data across a product’s lifecycle in a circular economic manner for all process steps until the delivery and disposal or re-use at the end customer. Also, product designers want to get insights about the feedback from the products to improve continuously. With the help of generative AI product engineers can develop new product ideas and requirements and visualize the product BOM natively connected to S/4HANA. This innovation empowers engineers to streamline product development and take efficiency to new heights. 

Business landscapes of companies are increasingly turning to market-driven decision-making for product innovation in order to drive growth and ensure long-term sustainability. One key tool that is revolutionizing this process is artificial intelligence (AI). By harnessing the power of AI, companies can analyze vast amounts of market data, consumer preferences, and trends to inform their product development strategies. AI-powered visualization tools are also playing a crucial role in enhancing collaboration and accelerating the product development cycles. These tools enable teams to seamlessly integrate data from product development with computer-aided design (CAD) models, resulting in improved efficiency and transparency throughout the manufacturing process.

A crucial aspect of market-driven decision-making is the concept of the Digital Thread. This refers to the integration of product design data throughout a product's entire lifecycle, from inception to disposal or re-use. By implementing a circular economic approach, companies can ensure that product design data is effectively utilized at every stage, leading to enhanced efficiency and reduced waste and emissions.

Furthermore, business AI is enabling product designers to gain valuable insights from customer feedback and continuously improve their offerings. Generative AI, in particular, is empowering engineers to develop innovative product ideas and requirements, while seamlessly connecting them to enterprise resource planning systems like S/4HANA. This groundbreaking innovation not only streamlines the product development process but also enables engineers to achieve unprecedented levels of efficiency.

In conclusion, leveraging AI and market-driven decision-making for product innovation is essential for companies looking to drive growth and sustainability. By embracing SAPs technologies and approaches, manufacturing businesses can stay ahead of the competition, deliver superior products, and meet the evolving needs of their customers.

Interested in learn more or discuss your business challenge?

Book a meeting with an expert from Pod1 (SAP Product Lifecycle Management) or Pod8 (Product Compliance for manufacturing).

Don't forget to explore the amazing showcases for process and discrete manufacturing industries - directly running on the booth to see how design data is linked to manufacturing and other processes.

Register for your free ticket here: https://events.sap.com/hannovermesse/en/home

Explore more about PLM solutions from SAP visit: https://www.sap.com/products/scm/plm-r-d-engineering.html

entry.title: Re: How to get SOA Manager Logical Port configuration Det...
entry.content: If this is a consumer webservice, then declare an struture of type of your consumer proxy (as seen in the image you have shared), and set your crendentials. I believe this authentication data is an input for your websrvice

entry.title: Implementation journey of SAP ECTR at OSG EX-CELL-O Coldforming Technologies
entry.content:
Sarah Mei Niesel of SAP PLM Cloud Acceleration and Jonathan Baechle from DSC Software AG share the successful implementation journey of SAP Engineering Control Center at OSG EX-CELL-O Coldforming Technologies, one of the world’s leading cutting tool manufacturers. In just 14 service days, they adeptly integrated the solution into the company's product development processes, setting a record.

SAP PLM played a crucial role as a foundation for integration, offering transparent and efficient product development operations. Despite strict deadlines and budget limits, OSG EX-CELL-O managed to overcome the hurdles and went live with the platform in record time. The quick rollout is attributed to the use of best practice configurations that were ready to use and the restructuring of PLM processes, which significantly boosted efficiency.

The implementation has positively overhauled operations at OSG EX-CELL-O, enabling the company to create project Bill of Materials (BOMs), establish data sharing across departments, and lay the groundwork for continuous product lifecycle management.

 

Read more about this story here!

For further insights into SAP Engineering Control Center and efficient product data management, read more success stories or refer to our product page on sap.com

Join our PLM Experts Forum on LinkedIn to stay connected with other PLM enthusiasts and check out our SAP PLM website at www.sap.com/plm

Find many more videos related to SAP PLM in our YouTube playlist and also check out The Power of PLM Part 1

entry.title: Replicate requirements from MD04 for multiple materials
entry.content:
Hi Have a list of 31 materials that I need to provide 24 months of requirements forecast for. 

Initially I went to SE16N - RESB and ran the data, then to validate via a pivot. 

However the totals do not appear to match MD04 when spot checking. 

Any others ideas of how I can get the requirements in total per material or Vendor ? 

Many Thanks 

TIA

entry.title: Re: Using both automatic overtime and approved overtime
entry.content:
Hi,

There is a grouping field on T510S - 'ORGMO Time wage type selection rule group'

This is set in standard PCR 'MODT' or your customer version of same. The setting of interest is MODIF W

This essentially allow you group your employees for wage type generation via T510S by what ever way best suits your business

Brendan

entry.title: Re: Can SAP's OData Framework be used as API endpoint?
entry.content:
SAP offers APIs that are specifically built for application-to-application communication, see https://api.sap.com/alltypes.

The majority of these APIs are OData APIs, and for S/4HANA they can be seen as successors of RFCs and BAPIs for synchronous A2A communication.

Building your own web APIs with RAP offers many out-of-the-box benefits that you don't get when directly using the underlying SICF, especially for following security standards.

entry.title: Re: How to add dynamically formcell or button table in Se...
entry.content: Thanks for the suggestion @bill_froelich , But what if i want to load the data based on the response I received from the backend as the current given data controls do not support the UI requirement? So what to do in that case ideally hiding them in the original page make them visible according to logic will be not be the ideal solution. Second, if we open the new page by adding dynamically as it is given in the question when we try to fetch the newly added controls using dynamic id but it does not give and pageref has the previous value(object) only.

entry.title: Re: generating qrcode using image url in crystal report
entry.content: Dear Experts, we have tried other API but it still doesn't show up

entry.title: Re: FB60 does not post. No error message is displayed.
entry.content:
You wrote 'When processing the batch session via SM35' use the available option in SM35 - two first options to display every or error dynpro

  • In fact, your BDC flow is missing a dynpro (check account/dc type field statis and other FI or CO required assignment) . Identify the case where this dynpro is needed and adapt the code that generates the DBC.
  • If you cannot find which one, you could use a tool that will generate the DBC itself, such as the FM POSTING_INTERFACE_DOCUMENT

entry.title: Re: Can SAP's OData Framework be used as API endpoint?
entry.content: Thank you, we'll try the RAP-OData after thomas' hint about the free-style web-service.

entry.title: iFlow ODATA POST API_CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV
entry.content:
Would you be able to help me with a problem I have with this iFlow I am trying to create.  This will eventually be for PDF documents to be attached to S4HC documents.
 
I can get the iFlow to add attachments to this SAP document - except the contents are not correct (it has the message mapping result).  But my question at this stage is why a direct call of the API via POSTMAN is not the same as when done using iFlow ODATA.
Here is the SAP document that I am attaching to.

 

The iFlow is using the ODATA adapter - and is using the API as seen in SAP_COM_0395
 
In the iFlow I am doing a GET of the attachment - I am not doing anything with this at this stage apart from setting the DocumentInfoRecordDocType (I will use more of it when I need to delete a document - but not worrying about that just now)
The Message Mapping source is the GET attachment and the target is the POST AttachmentContentSet
 
I can only get this iFlow to work if I have done the Message Mapping and the Content Modifier to set the API's headers.
I just do not understand why I need to set both as the message mapping sets more attributes than what I need if I call the API directly via POSTMAN
 
See this direct call of the SAP API - notice the 4 headers here are needed.  This will POST ok.

 

See how this matches what I needed to do with the iFlow Content Modifier

 

But in the iFlow I also need to do a message mapping otherwise it will not POST the ODATA call.  Notice there are more mandatory target attributes that must be set too
Why can my direct POST call of the API be only needing those 4 headers but the iFlow needing all of this too ?

entry.title: Re: Portfolio Management – Enhanced Financial Planning integration
entry.content:
Hi Andrea,

Very good and crisp summary of the all the innovations around financial planning in PPM . With the enablement of ACDOCP you can now also fully leverage in PPM all the new reporting and planning tools in FI/CO  that are based on ACDCOP.  A good example how S/4HANA core capabilities are continuously adopted in EPPM. This creates completely new options for planning and tracking costs throughout the entire project lifecycle

Hi,

Try adjusting table from Tcode SE14.

entry.title: Re: Can we install PaPM on-premise edition as an add-on o...
entry.content:
Hi Amogh,

From the installation perspective it should be possible to install OP addon on the private cloud edition. However, licensing could be something that you need to check.

I would recommend you to get in touch with the SAP account executive and they should be able to answer this better.

Regards,

Arun

entry.title: This logic is working for the gui.. how to get this field populated with app f3163 automatically ?
entry.content:
In the sapgui during BP create  the value of bu_group is copied to the field bpkind and augrp. With the fiori app F3163 we want to have the same during ‘submit’. How can that be done with a Tier3 enhancement or Tier2 wrapper? Of course, one should check if the ‘bu_group’ has been changed first (after creation it is not possible anymore to change this field/value anymore in our system).

thx in advance,

regards

entry.title: Re: What are the pre-requisite to get SAP B1 Partner for...
entry.content:
Hi Nikunj Mehta,

Your can start your journey here.

The requirements may have changed over the years, but it used to be that you needed a minimum of 1 sales person, 1 technical consultant, and 1 accountant, all certified for SAP Business One.

If you are interested in addon developement / sales only, you can also talk to an existing SAP Partner, and make some sort of freelance contract with them.

Regards,

Johan

entry.title: Subcontracting requirements does not consider plant's inventory
entry.content:
Hello,

I am writing this to seek for advices for my current scenario. I have a material with below condition:

1. Parent material A consists of component B

2. Material A I have maintained a valid purchasing info record with Auto Sourcing checked, and create a valid BOM that contains component B, as well as a valid production version

3. For component B, I have maintained the subcontracting MRP area (type 03) in the material master. In the MRP area, I maintained the special procurement type as '30'

Now that for component B, there is an inventory, says 1500 units in the storage location. And because of the POs of material A, there is a subcontracting requirement (MRP element 'SubReq') of component B, says 2000 unit shown in MD04. 

When MRP has executed, it proposed a PR with 2000 units to fulfill the SubReq. It does not consider the stock in the inventory.

So, I would like to understand, will there be a way to allow MRP to consider the inventory? Any advice is much appreciated

Thank you

entry.title: Re: Analytical Query Design with CDS - Restricted Key Figures - Hierarchy Nodes
entry.content:
Hello @joerg_franke , thank you for this blog, very helpful.

  1. for the hierarchy nodes, is there any way to display the text before the ID in the filter at the top ?
  2. Is there any possibility to hide the compound IDs of the hierarchy through annotations?

entry.title: Re: Error for Document Date in AR Invoice #59921
entry.content:
Hi  Krish_SAP

Please provide below details:

1) Can you update any other fields?

2) What is effected SAP Business One version ?

3) Does issue happen with all A/R invoices or with specific one ?

4) Can you reproduce same issue using demo database ?

Regards
Nidhi Singh
SAP Business One Support

entry.title: Re: How to get all logs or list of all events triggered t...
entry.content:
Hi @DhruvinM ,

you could start collecting trace data of your function calls and then introspect the traffic. The event gets dispatched to the Eventing module via the istio ingressgateway. The Eventing module then dispatches it to your function. These end-2-end request chains are trackable via traces.

Have a look at the SAP Cloud Logging integration guide to set up a Cloud Logging instance and integrate it with a Kyma runtime for trace collection.

Hope that helps,

Andreas

entry.title: Re: generating qrcode using image url in crystal report
entry.content: Dear Expert, Where can we find that UFL is it available in crystal report? do we have to install it first or is it already there? Thank You

entry.title: Re: What is shown under 'Latest replies'?
entry.content:
Hi @Johan_H and @TuncayKaraca, 

What you see in the Latest Replies section is the main thread/main content with the most recent reply, not the latest reply itself. 

The platform will also surface content that is not necessarily tagged with the SAP Managed tag, but content where the product is mentioned.
For instance the second reply on the SAP Business One page here is not tagged, but is still relevant for Business One:

entry.title: Re: implementation partner is different and license partn...
entry.content:
Hi Nikunj,

This is allowed, but only one of the partners is responsible for the support. This is usually the partner you pay the support fee to.

The partner who provides the support, will probably have an opinion about this scenario.

Regards,

Johan

entry.title: Access to sample datasets used in SAP Teched
entry.content:
Hello,

Is there any way that we can access all the sample datasets used in the teched sap sessions?
The Github link only has the the steps that are meant to be followed but there was no sample dataset present in the page.

Kindly help with this query.
Thank You

entry.title: Re: DI Server/API Encounters XCE208 busy and OBServerDLL...
entry.content:
Hi allenzhang

The "XCE208 Resource is busy" means that there's no DI Proxy available to serve a scenario step request. You can increase the number of DI Proxies in a DI Proxy Group to try and reduce this.

For how to setup multiple DI Proxies and DI Groups, please refer to section 9 in NOTE 2130388.

For memory allocate to TOMCAT, you can check Maximum memory pool allocated to B1i (tomcat):  ..\SAP\SAP Business One Integration\IntegrationServer\Tomcat\bin\tomcat8w.exe and find the field on Java tab and increase the Maximum memory pool  value.

Restart SAP Business One Integration service after this.

Kind Regards
Nidhi Singh
SAP Business One Support

entry.title: Re: What is shown under 'Latest replies'?
entry.content:
Hi @anne-petteroe,

It seems to me that this does not work correctly. At the very least the logic does not match with common expectations. Sorry, I am not sure what you mean with: "...the main thread/main content...".

The first entry is still there, while similar other questions have had more recent replies. Same goes for the last one. In fact there are more recent replies with the SAP Business One tag or mention, any which way you throw it.

Maybe the second one has the word SAP Business One in a reply, somewhere, but I cannot find it. For this element to be usefull in this case, the link should open up the comment in question. Now it opens up to a random reply:

Please note, that these two replies do not have the SAP Business One tag nor is it mentioned in the texts. The only common denominator is the word SAP. If that is all that it takes, there maybe something wrong with the algorithm.

Regards,

Johan

entry.title: Re: Missing graph labels and legend in webi
entry.content:
The problem affects only Linux BO servers and it was due to an specific type of font that needs to be removed from the server

https://me.sap.com/notes/3373089

Cause:

The OpenType Font (.OTF) fonts may generate bad calculations for text with SAP JVM, expecially on Suse distributions.

This type of font must be removed from the system in order not to override the default behavior which uses TTF fonts.

 

These command lines help to detect and remove all OTF files from the system.

Execute them from a Shell command:

  • find /  -type f  -name "*.otf"

If the command detects fonts, execute the following commands:

  • find /  -type f  -name "*.otf" -exec /bin/rm -i {} \;
    •  type "y" for each entry only if it's a font (pay attention to the file location e.g. /truetype/)
  • Update the font cache: fc-cache -f -v
  • Restart the APS hosting the Visualization Service.

entry.title: How to do client copy in SAP BW after database content refresh
entry.content:
We created a homogenous system copy by backup/restore from our Production BW (Netweaver 7.5) to Quality BW (Netweaver 7.5). At the same time we also did a system copy from our ERP Production to the ERP Quality. The productive clients in BW PRD and BW QUA differ (300 in PRD and 100 in QUA). After the system copy we wanted to keep the QUA system as close to the system before copy and wanted to create a client copy from QUA.300 to QUA.100 but it seems like this process is not supported if we want to retain all the data in the post-copy QUA system.

We followed the scenario B2 in note 886102 (https://me.sap.com/notes/886102) but even when we go through all the steps in the target system and running BDLSS we still get the error "Logical System name has been changed for this system". Is there something important not mentioned in the note that has to be done in addition? Should we delete some configuration before changing the logical system for the target system client?

Best Regards,

Rauno

entry.title: Re: Error Please Check ROUTE and SLOC
entry.content:
This is not a standard SAP error message - but some specific enhancement which only exists in your system. hence you need to check with your IT department to check on the specific logic behind it.

Furthermore OX09 is customzing and you should not be changing storage location adresses in a productive environment as a work-around.

entry.title: Re: April (Citizen) Developer Challenge – SAP Build Apps: Task 3

entry.title: Re: generating qrcode using image url in crystal report
entry.content:

thank you

entry.title: Re: SAP PI - Determine the receiver based on Source FileName
entry.content:
Yes, I tested that and it didnt work.

Also I tested it with different Xpath statements and it is still not working. The latest Xpath Statement I used and its not working is this:

[not(contains(ContextObject.FileName, '12345'))]

And I use it as this in the editor:

Any suggestions?

entry.title: Re: Replicate requirements from MD04 for multiple materials
entry.content:
Hello @mikegaunt 

RESB holds reservation items. Reservations contribute to overall requirements but they are not all requirements, as you can have also other requirements like customer orders.

One of the ways to report requirements would be the MC47 transaction.

Another a custom report over stock/requirements list that can be read with the BAPI_MATERIAL_STOCK_REQ_LIST function.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: Dates and Roundings in Compensation
entry.content:
Hello @xavierlegarrec @pmacgovern 

Any thoughts on my query above.

entry.title: Re: Error Please Check ROUTE and SLOC
entry.content:
Hello @gntze 

Ask the user to double click on the error message and get the error message number. That would help a lot in analyzing the issue. 

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: SAC Story Filters: Advanced Filtering with BW Live Data
entry.content: Hello Tuncay, thanks for your further explanations and sources for filtering! 🙂 The thing I meant, how SAP thinks that a user can creat an advanced filter by clicking in the widgets? Certainly I can click on a data point of a bar chart e.g. and with linked analysis I also could create perhaps some more complex filters by that. But from my point of view I do not see a chance to create a filter by doing this with the complexity of a real advanced filter as it is possible with an import model.

entry.title: Kyma Integration with SAP Cloud Logging. Part 2: Let's ship some traces
entry.content:
In the first blog post of this three-part series, we talked about:

  • What is SAP Cloud Logging?
  • How to integrate it with Kyma runtime?
  • How to ship logs?

In this blog, we will explore:

  • Shipping traces from Kyma runtime to SAP Cloud Logging.
  • Using SAP Cloud Logging dashboard to view and leverage traces.

What is distributed tracing?

Distributed tracing is a method used to monitor and profile applications that span multiple services or components. It provides a detailed view of the flow of requests as they traverse through various parts of a distributed system. By capturing and correlating information about each request, distributed tracing enables developers and operators to understand the performance characteristics, latency bottlenecks, and dependencies within their systems.

Shipping traces from Kyma runtime to SAP Cloud Logging

SAP Cloud Logging based on OpenSearch supports ingestion of traces when having the OTLP ingestion mode enabled. It provides pre-integrated dashboards to introspect them easily.

The goal of the next steps is to establish a cluster-central gateway using the Kyma Telemetry module being connected to Cloud Logging.

Additionally, we enable Istio to propagate trace context for every incoming request and emit trace data to the central gateway. Also, we enable our application to instrument and emit trace data to the gateway.

Prerequisites

  • SAP BTP, Kyma runtime instance
  • Telemetry module enabled in Kyma runtime instance
  • SAP Cloud Logging Instance, Blog 1
  • kubectl and command line support

Steps

  • Configure Trace pipeline.

You must tell Kyma to ship the generated traces to the SAP Cloud Logging instance. This is done by creating a Trace pipeline. If you followed steps in the previous blog post, then you can simply run the following command, else adjust the name for secretKeyRef accordingly.

 

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/SAP-samples/kyma-runtime-extension-samples/main/sap-cloud-logging/k8s/tracing/traces-pipeline.yaml

 

For reference, this is what the TracePipeline looks like:

 

apiVersion: telemetry.kyma-project.io/v1alpha1
kind: TracePipeline
metadata:
  name: my-cls-trace-pipeline
spec:
  output:
    otlp:
      endpoint:
        valueFrom:
          secretKeyRef:
            name: my-cls-binding
            namespace: cls
            key: ingest-otlp-endpoint
      tls:
        cert:
          valueFrom:
            secretKeyRef:
              name: my-cls-binding
              namespace: cls
              key: ingest-otlp-cert
        key:
          valueFrom:
            secretKeyRef:
              name: my-cls-binding
              namespace: cls
              key: ingest-otlp-key

 

To learn more about TracePipeline and possible configuration options, check the resource documentation.

  • Next, you enable mesh level configuration using Istio Telemetry resource.

 

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/SAP-samples/kyma-runtime-extension-samples/main/sap-cloud-logging/k8s/tracing/trace-istio-telemetry.yaml

 

For reference, this is how the default telemetry configuration looks like:

 

apiVersion: telemetry.istio.io/v1alpha1
kind: Telemetry
metadata:
  name: tracing-default
  namespace: istio-system
spec:
  tracing:
  - providers:
    - name: "kyma-traces"
    randomSamplingPercentage: 1.0

 

You can learn more about various options in the standard Istio documentation.

With this configuration, you enable tracing 1.0% of all requests that enter the Kyma runtime via Istio Service mesh. To optimize resource and network usage, it is important to set the sampling percentage value to a sensible default.

To understand how tracing is implemented in Kyma and what its architecture looks like, check the Kyma documentation.

NOTE: This would require an SAP Cloud Logging service instance. To find out how to create it, read the first blog post.

Viewing the traces

You can access the SAP Cloud Logging instance dashboard. The access details are available in the Secret generated by the service binding.

Next, to access the traces, you can navigate to:

Observability --> Trace Analytics --> Traces

You can also use various filter criteria to narrow down to the trace you are particularly interested in. The simplest one to use is the Service Name. It is the name of the Kubernetes Service which is associated with your microservice or function.

In case the response contains a request ID, you can always use it to find the corresponding Trace ID from the logs and use that as filter criteria.

This is particularly useful if you have enabled 100% tracing and want to troubleshoot a specific request.

Here is an example of using the request-id for a CAP application.

 

Examples

Here are some examples of how traces look for various scenarios:

A function calling an upstream microservice:

 

I would like to point out that Kyma serverless does the additional instrumentation of your function code for tracing, and the same is visible in the spans generated.

One microservice calling another upstream microservice:

 

A multi-tenant JavaScript CAP application:

 

CAP JavaScript supports instrumentation. You just need to add the following NPM packages to your CAP project, provided by SAP and open telemetry.

 

{
  "dependencies": {
    "@cap-js/telemetry": "^0.1.0",
    "@grpc/grpc-js": "^1.10.3",
    "@opentelemetry/exporter-metrics-otlp-grpc": "^0.49.1",
    "@opentelemetry/exporter-trace-otlp-grpc": "^0.49.1",
    .....
  }
}

 

You can learn more about cap-js/telemetry in npm package documentation as well as GitHub repository.

Instrumentation

As mentioned in the previous example, you can use the npm package cap-js/telemetry for a JavaScript-based CAP application. For other technologies, consider using the SDKs provided by OpenTelemetry.

Cloud Native Buildpacks + Java

If you are using cloud native buildpacks, then you can also consider enabling OpenTelemetry while building the docker image. Check out this example from OpenTelemetry. There is also information about Paketo Buildpack for OpenTelemetry

NOTE: The endpoints to which the traces and metrics need to be shipped are available in the Telemetry default custom resource. You can view them in kyma-system namespace under Kyma -> Telemetry

Istio sidecar trace generation

If you have Istio sidecar enabled, Istio takes care of generating Spans, linking them to traces when you make upstream calls to services in the Istio service mesh. The requirement is to pass the appropriate headers so Istio can do the linking and generate the trace / span details.

This can be achieved by:

  • Using one of the SDKs mentioned above. (Preferred option)
  • Or, manually passing the required headers.

Reference: Istio distributed tracing

If you would like to do manual propagation, you should take the incoming "x-request-id" and "traceparent" headers from the HTTP request and forward them when making upstream calls to other microservices running inside the same Kyma cluster and part of Istio service mesh.

Other scenarios

Similarly, for calls to DB e.g. to SAP HANA Cloud, you can consider using SDKs that do the instrumentation.

How can you benefit from using distributed tracing?

Distributed tracing is a powerful technique used to monitor and understand the behavior of complex distributed systems. By implementing distributed tracing, you can gain several benefits:

  1. Troubleshooting and debugging: Distributed tracing allows you to track requests as they flow through various components of your system. This helps identify performance bottlenecks, latency issues, and errors, making it easier to troubleshoot and debug problems.

  2. Performance optimization: With distributed tracing, you can analyze the timing and dependencies of requests across different services. This helps identify areas where performance improvements can be made, such as optimizing slow database queries, reducing network latency, or optimizing resource usage.

  3. Service dependency visualization: Distributed tracing provides a holistic view of how different services interact with each other. This helps understand the dependencies between services and identify potential points of failure or bottlenecks.

  4. Capacity planning: By analyzing the traces of requests, you can gain insights into the resource utilization patterns of your system. This can help in capacity planning, ensuring that your system is provisioned with the right amount of resources to handle the expected load.

Coming next

Stay tuned for the next blog on Shipping metrics to SAP Cloud Logging from Kyma runtime.

entry.title: Re: How to get all logs or list of all events triggered t...
entry.content:
Hi @DhruvinM ,

in the eventing-CR you could change the Log-Level to Debug. That will print much more information about whats going on in the eventing system.

To check for incoming events you would then have to check the logs of the eventing-publisher-proxy deployment.

Remember to disable this feature asap, as it generates many more logs than you would probably need.

the configuration is mentioned here. The configuration field in question is logging.logLevel.
Set its value to

```
logging:
      logLevel:  "debug"
```

 

Cheers,
  Korbinian

entry.title: Re: Replicate requirements from MD04 for multiple materials
entry.content: Is there a way to configure MC47 for qty ?

entry.title: Re: GTS Billing document of orders that are blocked should be prevented
entry.content: I am also looking at this. How do you prevent sales order related invoices from being generated?

entry.title: SAP Fiori Frontend 6.0 App installation and connection to SAP Business Suite
entry.content:
Hello there,

I got recently the request that my HCM team want to access their bank details via Fiori app. Since we are still on ECC and not on HANA, I chose the Fiori Front end Server solution.

I installed the Fiori server and successfully set up the connection to the HCM system ( trusted connection, SSO, distribute the permission for the users, local aliases, etc.) 

The setup is the following:

FES

Backendsystem (ABAP HCM)

Nothing special installed for the Fiori setup.

---------

Now comes the difficult part for me: I already can see the fiori app "bank details". When i click on the app, i get an error that an odata service is missing (/sap/opu/odata/sap/HCMFAB_COMMON_SRV/$metadata).

The odata service can not be found in /N/IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE.

When I have a look in the Fiori-App-Library, I can see as requirement that I need as product Version in the backend system SAP Fiori 2.0 for SAP ERP HCM + GBX01HR5 605 - SP 0024. ( Requirements for the Fiori Front end are met in my opinion)

Our Solman (aggregate) also suggest to install the marked green components.

My questions are now:

1. Do I need to install SAP Fiori 2.0 for SAP ERP HCM + GBX01HR5 605 - SP 0024 on the HCM Backend server? When I do this, i wont have the necessary odata service at the FES, but i should activate this one. Does that even work?

 

2. Could it be possible to install the GBX01HR5 605 component on the FES ( I mean, then i would also need the whole EA-HR packages...)

3. The GBX01HR5 software component is part of the SAP Fiori 2.0 for SAP ERP HCM product version - did I understand that correctly?

 

Sorry for the long text.

 

Regards

Florian

entry.title: Creating a JDBC Connection for Google BigQuery using SSO with Oauth 2.0
entry.content:
Hi all,

We're going to set up a JDBC connection for Google Big Query.

For Security reasons we will use SSO with Oauth 2.0 :

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_BUSINESSOBJECTS_BUSINESS_INTELLIGENCE_PLATFORM/aa4cb9ab429349e49678e146f05d7341/c9b3f585700a4183af9e8a3735c757bb.html?locale=en-US&version=4.3.3

Do you have specific documentation related to this.

Most of the document are refering to authentication using a json file:

3413728 - Where to find OAuth 2.0 Configuration Information For BI 4.3+

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/what-is-all-about-authorization-server-oauth-sso-introduced-in-sap/ba-p/13530280

Do you have some hints for configuring  OAuth and Authorization Server Configuration on GCP ?

The main goal is not to schedule to GCP, we have migrated our data to GCP and we want to set up a connection on IDT using a jdbc driver to connect to GCP.

 

Regards

entry.title: Re: Dates and Roundings in Compensation
entry.content:
4793/5326 = 0.8999248967. If you round that, you get 1, so that is why you get 100%. You need to multiply by 100, round, then divide by 100.

0.8999248967 * 100 = 89.99248967, rounded becomes 90.0 and divide by 100 to get 0.9, which is 90%.

Use

round('halfUp',(customTeamBonus/customTarget)*100)/100

entry.title: Re: No authorization to create tasklist for plant (genera...
entry.content:
Hi,

run SU53 tcode and consultant basis team for missing authorization object.

Regards

entry.title: What’s New in SAP Build – Seamless Integration of Processes and Apps
entry.content:
Greetings Builders!

We’re pleased to announce this major feature release for our low-code portfolio SAP Build. It is now possible to trigger and consume processes from SAP Build Process Automation directly in SAP Build Apps.

By bridging the gap between the two products, this new capability is a significant step towards a more unified experience across SAP Build. Previously, the primary way to establish a connection was via APIs – the process is described in this blog. Now when developing applications and automations, the integration steps have been simplified to a smooth transition, allowing developers to get deeper insights into process and decision scenarios while enabling a native mobile or web experience.

How to get started

Before getting started with this feature in your projects, we recommend looking at the guide Best Practices: A Strategic Approach to SAP Build Apps. While the low-code approach allows both citizen and professional developers to get started quickly, it’s important to understand optimal approaches, especially for data integrations, to create enterprise apps and extensions that are both scalable and secure.

SAP BTP prerequisites

You will need a BTP Subaccount with service assignments for both SAP Build Apps and SAP Build Process Automation under your entitlements. See the initial setup guides for both:

  • Subscribe to SAP Build Apps
  • Subscribe to SAP Build Process Automation

Setting up the integration

👉 For the complete steps to set up this integration, see the Help Portal.

Important note:

  • When configuring destinations in the SAP BTP Cockpit, check your service destination (technical name: sap_process_automation_service_user_access) and make sure you have added the following properties: AppgyverEnabled – true, and MobileEnabled – true. This is mainly for subaccounts that were previously provisioned with SAP Build Process Automation via booster. For new ones, the booster should add these properties automatically.

To start discovering and utilizing processes within your applications, you will need to create at least one Business Process and release it in SAP Build Process Automation.

Check out this blog from Rama Murari to explore these steps with a use case for releasing credit blocked sales orders in SAP S/4HANA.

You will find your published processes in the SAP Build Library, which is a new integration option for connecting SAP Build products. Once you enable the selected process, you can begin to leverage it in your apps by adding a logic function that will trigger the process.

Important notes:

  • Only processes with an API Trigger are visible in the SAP Build Library.
  • SAP Build Apps can only access the latest published process version. Make sure to always publish the newest version to the library.

After completing the integration steps, you can test it out with the preview app to see that your process is running. In the SAP Build Lobby, you can also confirm your process is successfully triggered with the exact inputs entered into the app!

👉 See the complete steps for this feature in the Help Portal.

We’re excited to make this feature available to our customers as it brings SAP Build closer together with a more harmonized low-code experience. Let us know your thoughts and feedback in the comments.

Happy Building! 🥳🤝

***

▫️Check out what else is on the horizon this year in the Roadmap Explorer: SAP Build Apps and SAP Build Process Automation

▫️ For all recent releases, see What’s New: SAP Build Apps and SAP Build Process Automation

▫️Stay connected with the latest in SAP Build: Builders Group, LinkedIn, YouTube

**Disclaimer 19-4-2024: There is currently an issue affecting users in US10. We will update this when it is resolved. EU10 is operational.**

entry.title: Re: What are the pre-requisite to get SAP B1 Partner for...
entry.content: Hi, What is the process for that? like Where need to register first time?

entry.title: Re: Taking Data Federation to the Next Level: Accessing Remote ABAP CDS View Entities in SAP HANA Cl
entry.content:
Very Nice new Feature for Hana Cloud, that simplifies the usage of ABAP ODBC greatly.   😎

One downside of the ABAP ODBC approach, at the moment, is that it  seems to rely on additional custom CDS views and SQL Service bindings being built to selectively expose 'Released for Cloud Development' CDS views. It's not quite a simple as just connecting to the those CDS views directly via Datasphere (SDI approach).

BTW It also appears that the Hana script server needs to be active, which is only avaialble starting with 48Gb Mem instances, so not an option on small trial environments sadly.

https://help.sap.com/docs/hana-cloud/sap-hana-cloud-administration-guide/sap-hana-database-additional-features?locale=en-US&q=script%20server

entry.title: SAP B1 Service layer: windows service permissions
entry.content:
Hello,

I am trying to POST attachment via Service Layer to SAP B1 attachments folder. The shared folder is on network drive, so standard Service Layer windows service user "Local System" is not able to reach it and return error 500 "Internal server error". We have created special "service user" under which we would like window's services for service layer to run. This user is given permissions to network folder, however when we set this user under windows service "Log on as" - windows services failing to start. In windows evens log error description just says "Incorrect function" which does not really help to understand.

While testing, if I change "log on as" for Windows Services (SL Balancer and nodes) to use my full access admin account, everything works. Also, if I add the new "service user" to Local admin group - then it works as well. So it's apparently permissions issue on local server where SAP B1 Service layer (and SLD) are installed. We do not like to have this "service user" in Local admin group, so question - anybody knows what permissions are actually required for windows user that is running SAP B1 Service layer services?

entry.title: Re: Subcontracting requirements does not consider plant's inventory
entry.content: would you share screen shots of MD04 of the component B in plant and MRP areas?

entry.title: Re: In NWDS when exporting mapping to spreadsheet the constant values are not visible
entry.content: Hi Danny, our NWDS shows the same, only const() without value. But if I use the ESB and select the text preview it looks similar to the spreadsheet export and their is an value in the const()

entry.title: Re: Replicate requirements from MD04 for multiple materials
entry.content: @mikegaunt Once MC47 list is displayed, click "Double-line" button. That will get you quantities.

entry.title: Re: How to do client copy in SAP BW after database conten...
entry.content:
The error message means that you already changed (on t-code SCC4 perhaps) the LOGICAL SYSTEM name before running the BDLS. This is not allowed. The change should be done only through the BDLS t-code, no matter if it is a ERP or a BW.

Because you did also a system copy of the ERP, the more appropriate scenario is the A2, because you copied the BW and the source system (ERP).

entry.title: Activation of IAS
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We have Azure setup as the Corporate IDP where users will be logging through Azure via SSO and then redirected to SF via IAS as proxy. There are few consultants who will be logging via Password.

The default Identity Provider is set as Microsoft Azure(Allow users stored in Identity Authentication service to log on was enabled) . When we started to test activation via password user, it directed me to the Azure screen. Deleted cookies but still same behavior. I had to switch the default identity provider to "Identity Authentication" then it worked( We have conditional authentication rule setup- 1 as PWD and other as SSO). Later to test activation for SSO users, had to set the identity provider back to Azure. Just want to check is this expected behavior. 

Please share if anyone has experienced this issue and is normal behavior.

Thanks

Regards

Vidya

entry.title: Meet PLM experts from SAP at Hannover Messe 2024
entry.content:
Business landscapes of companies are increasingly turning to market-driven decision-making for product innovation in order to drive growth and ensure long-term sustainability. One key tool that is revolutionizing this process is artificial intelligence (AI). By harnessing the power of AI, companies can analyze vast amounts of market data, consumer preferences, and trends to inform their product development strategies. AI-powered visualization tools are also playing a crucial role in enhancing collaboration and accelerating the product development cycles. These tools enable teams to seamlessly integrate data from product development with computer-aided design (CAD) models, resulting in improved efficiency and transparency throughout the manufacturing process.

A crucial aspect of market-driven decision-making is the concept of the Digital Thread. This refers to the integration of product design data throughout a product's entire lifecycle, from inception to disposal or re-use. By implementing a circular economic approach, companies can ensure that product design data is effectively utilized at every stage, leading to enhanced efficiency and reduced waste and emissions.

Furthermore, business AI is enabling product designers to gain valuable insights from customer feedback and continuously improve their offerings. Generative AI, in particular, is empowering engineers to develop innovative product ideas and requirements, while seamlessly connecting them to enterprise resource planning systems like S/4HANA. This groundbreaking innovation not only streamlines the product development process but also enables engineers to achieve unprecedented levels of efficiency.

In conclusion, leveraging AI and market-driven decision-making for product innovation is essential for companies looking to drive growth and sustainability. By embracing SAPs technologies and approaches, manufacturing businesses can stay ahead of the competition, deliver superior products, and meet the evolving needs of their customers.

Interested in learn more or discuss your business challenge?

Book a meeting with an expert from Pod1 (SAP Product Lifecycle Management) or Pod8 (Product Compliance for manufacturing).

Don't forget to explore the amazing showcases for process and discrete manufacturing industries - directly running on the booth to see how design data is linked to manufacturing and other processes.

Register for your free ticket here: https://events.sap.com/hannovermesse/en/home

Explore more about PLM solutions from SAP visit: https://www.sap.com/products/scm/plm-r-d-engineering.html

entry.title: SAP BTP, Kyma Runtime internally available on SAP Converged Cloud
entry.content:
Hello Kyma community,

the Kyma team is happy to announce the SAP internal availability of SAP BTP, Kyma runtime on SAP Converged Cloud in the eu-de-1 region. This is the first stage of gradual enablement in non-restricted regions that should culminate in the general availability of Kyma on SAP Converged Cloud for all SAP customers. With that, we will do our part to deliver the SAP BTP Everywhere promise. 

We started on this journey almost two years ago. The feedback we received from a limited pilot early last year guided our efforts towards delivering the best possible user experience to enable our customers to seamlessly adopt and benefit from SAP Converged Cloud. In turn, we ensure that our customers can start their migration efforts or new development today without additional effort or cost compared to major hyperscalers. 

Right now, you can simply enable Kyma on SAP Converged Cloud via the SAP BTP Cockpit and start using the SAP BTP platform in the SAP Datacenter just like you would on the other hyperscalers. There is no need to manage infrastructure accounts and quotas, install and upgrade Kubernetes, or worry about infrastructure monitoring and security scanning. The Kyma team handles all day two operations related to infrastructure and available modules, so you do not have to. 

You can immediately connect your applications to most SAP BTP services (from the nearby cf-eu20 region today) or SAP HANA Cloud instances even if the whole of BTP is not running on SAP Converged Cloud yet. We made sure that all currently available Kyma modules run smoothly and do not require specific configuration for SAP Converged Cloud. 

There are some limitations we are working on. For example, it is not currently possible to set up the hyperscaler equivalent of VPC peering within or outside SAP Converged Cloud. Nor is it possible to connect to HANA Cloud instances via private networking (which is the case on hyperscalers as well). 

Stay tuned for more updates, and give Kyma on SAP Converged Cloud a try in the meantime! 

To learn more about SAP BTP, Kyma runtime please read and follow our other blogs, and visit the SAP BTP, Kyma runtime site.

entry.title: Re: What are the pre-requisite to get SAP B1 Partner for...
entry.content: Please use the link in my answer. It will take you to the SAP PartnerEdge page. You will find more information there on what to do, and if there are any costs attached..

entry.title: Re: How to search batches on the basis of a remaining shelf life in batch determination?
entry.content:
hi

thanks and i followed your indications. However, the batches are still "Incomplete" when checking with MSC3N.

i have to admit that the classification in the materials the Class that is assigned also includes the LOBM-RLZ. We try to delete the Characteristic assignment with report RMCLMDEL. Here we are struggling finding the right Change Number. In any case, i would expect it NOT to be an issue that LOBM-RLZ is part of the Class assigned in the material (class type 023 in material master).

for now we try to go the way of deleting the assignment with the report. Any other suggestions are still welcome though

entry.title: Re: [BIG PROBLEM] SAP Host Agent cannot connect to SYSTEMDB
entry.content:
Hello,

Could you please elaborate on symptom, what problem you see, where. Appeciate that you are trying to avoid to reinvent the wheel and so listed your troubleshotting steps but it seems to confuse things, at least for me. I am not sure the screenshot that you havegiven is running what command from where. Also, is it secure connection (HTTPS) between Host agent and HANA? If yes, have both, agent and HANA got same root certificate installed or trust established by any other mans?

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Uploading Targets with the extended "Manage ESG Data" App
entry.content: Solved: The problem was Microsoft Excel because it changed the formating automatically.

entry.title: Re: Unable to configure SAP CC Catalog webservice for the...
entry.content:
Hi Anil,

SOM communicates with CC only over the SOAP WS API so, from CC's point of view, you have to configure only web service entry points.
To make sure that everything is properly set up on SOM's side, can you please involve a SOM expert?

In the meantime, you might also want to verify whether there are communication errors between SOM and CC.
You can do this either by checking the error logs in SOM, or by activating the WS debug traces in the CC updater.
If you observe any abnormal message there, feel free to let me (and/or the SOM experts) know.

Best regards.

François
SAP Convergent Charging Support

entry.title: Re: Change header field of purchase order on save
entry.content: Thanks, as I stated in my initial post, I already tried my code (see above) in method CHECK, but value is NOT updated in DB table.

entry.title: Re: Error for Document Date in AR Invoice #59921
entry.content:
Hi,

Please check SAP Note 3366711 which looks to be something recorded on similar lines. 

Check by upgrading the COPY of the database to FP2311 or higher and check if the error appears on it.

Kr,

Jitin

If anyone can help!

entry.title: Re: When to use Event Mesh Webhook
entry.content: Excellent Blog

entry.title: Error by Logon to the SAP Cloud System
entry.content:
Hello SAP Comunity, 

I have a free Trail account . I am currently facing a problem while trying to log on my ABAP Cloud project in eclipse. I am getting the following error : Error while logging on the service instance using this project. Please make sure that you are logging on with : my email address. Although I simultaneously get the following message im Browser:  You have been successfully logged on: You can close this page and continue in the ABAP Development Tools. 

The trial account has only one E-mail Address. 

Yesterday, it was working perfectly but I tried the wohle day to relog in eclipse , restart it , restart windows, deleted the data in cache. But nothing seems to work unfortuantly. 

Thanks in advance

Best regards 

sisi

entry.title: Re: implementation partner is different and license partn...
entry.content:
Hi,

You can check your question and concerns with the Solution Expert for your localization. Details of the same can be obtained from below link:

https://partneredge.sap.com/en/library/assets/products/ent_mng/sbo/about/sbo/versions/pp_so_sbo_ctryavail.html

Kr,

Jitin

entry.title: Re: Change header field of purchase order on save
entry.content:
Try to prevent infinite loop, change your code to smothing such as 

* Get header data
ls_header = im_header->get_data( ).
* Prevent infinite loop
if ls_header-unsez ne text-100.
* Set value
  ls_header-unsez = text-100.
* Set header data
  im_header->set_data( ls_header ).
  im_header->set_changed( ).
endif.

entry.title: Re: April (Citizen) Developer Challenge – SAP Build Apps: Task 3

Hash: 5a631a765d1e6e35de8f71a7550c5e6e984c6d295b1a0a3c8fb4526f31f6cb3c

Screenshots:

Home screen 
Details screen

entry.title: Edit Query Disabled from File server data source in Data Management SAC
entry.content:
Hello,

Edit Query Disabled from file server data source in Data Management SAC.

I have full control on Model and File server connection was shared with Edit rights to my profile. 

This is analytical model. Not sure why I am unable to edit the existing import job query.

Regards,

Vasu

entry.title: Re: Use of Sequence into SAP B1 / SAP Business One
entry.content: hi, Attached sap note is quite different as per actual question. SAP Note is relevant with 3394832 - Table Primary Keys are not consecutive

entry.title: Re: What are the pre-requisite to get SAP B1 Partner for...
entry.content:
Hi,

I am not sure whether you are looking to Find a Partner. if yes, may be below link could give you some information:

https://partneredge.sap.com/en/partnership/marketing/campaigns/part-find.html

Kr,

Jitin

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud for planning - Resource Issue for Data Action
entry.content:
HI friends,

right now Iam facing the Issue that a Data Action fails because of not enough resources. 

The data action "DataAction_Name" couldn't run on version "Version_Name" because you don't have the resources available for step 1. Try restricting the scope of this step or try again later.

In this post this issue was already mentioned.

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/data-action-fails-sporadically-not-enough-resources-available-try-again/qaq-p/12713612 

Is there a way to track the used resources in the SAC?

Kind regards,

Ismail

entry.title: Re: How to do client copy in SAP BW after database conten...
entry.content:
Yes sorry, that was an unintentional typo. I meant A2.

It's good that you mentioned the order. Indeed I set the logical system for the client before running BDLS. Trying it the other way around and will update with the results.

entry.title: Re: NF-e Nota Técnica 2023.004 v1.10: novos campos adicionados à nota fiscal
entry.content:
Ola Luize ... implementamos as SNOTES informadas e segundo o forum aqui e segundo a SNOTE o campo indDeduzDeson não iria ver preenchido, o preenchimento do mesmo fica sob responsabilidade do cliente de colocar a lógica via desenvolvimento, correto?

porém, pegamos situações aqui em que o campo está vindo preenchido de forma automatica, pela própria solução standard, o caso de STO UB movto 862/861 e o caso de SUBContract movto 542/541 - o campo vem preenchido com 0, que segundo o manual abaixo, ele deve ser informado quando houver o valor vICMSDeson e o mesmo não reduzir do valor do item ...

entendemos que isso é um ERRO e não deveria ocorrer, o nosso sistema que esta incorreto? pq não fizemos ainda nenhum desenvolvimento para este preenchimento e ele seta este valor automatico de = 0 para estes cenários, e apenas complementando no caso da STO UB meu cenário possui ICMS CST 41 sem Deson.

como devemos proceder? entendo que futuramente para entrega das obrigações este campo poderá ser validado e na solucao estará preenchido indevidamente.

Obrigado

entry.title: Re: Revaluation of Batch Item Variance Recognition
entry.content:
Hi,

Please check Revaluation Topic from Page 78 onwards and compare your transaction with the example provided and see if that is what has happened on your side as well:

https://help.sap.com/doc/DRAFT/a9dab1de606f4c71a51c19b6e26f6cb0/10.0/en-US/How_to_Set_Up_and_Manage_a_Perpetual_Inventory_System_10.0.pdf

Kr,

Jitin

entry.title: Re: Fiscal calendar generation for SAP Datasphere using built-in procedure
entry.content:
Thanks a lot for this blog.

I would say SAP Datasphere should provide this Fiscal Variants related content with Time Tables and Dimensions Or as a Standard content just like currency conversion tables have been provided. Fiscal calendar is highly used objects as we all know specially doing FI reporting.

Hope SAP will provide Fiscal calendar content in upcoming content release.

entry.title: SAP Developer News, April 18th, 2024
entry.content:
Podcast Version: SAP Developer News April 18th, 2024 – SAP Developers (opensap.info)

Description:

SAP CodeJam European Roadshow 

  • SAP CodeJam Event Calendar: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-codejam/eb-p/codejam-events 
  • SAP CodeJam Group: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-codejam/gh-p/code-jam 

 SAP CodeTalk with Daniel Schlachter on the CAP plugin concept 

  • The interview https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=cG-cMqAhqyQ  
  • The Capire section on "CAP Plugins & Enhancements" https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/plugins/#cap-plugins-enhancements  

SAP Build Developer Challenge Week 3 Announcement 

  • SAP builders group discussion : https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-builders-discussions/april- citizen-developer-challenge-sap-build-apps-task-3/td-p/13672860 

FioriConf 2024 and SAP CodeJam – SAP Fiori elements flexible programming model 

  • FioriConf 2024: https://www.fioriconf.org/ 
  • SAP CodeJam topic list: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-codejam-blog-posts/sap-codejam-topics/ba-p/221407 
  • SAP CodeJam – SAP Fiori elements flexible programming model: https://github.com/SAP-samples/fiori-elements-fpm-exercises-codejam 

Solution Experience Live Session: Deliver Real-World Results with SAP Business AI, April 30, 2024 

  • Register: https://events.sap.com/live-session-sap-business-ai/en/home 
  • SAP Business AI release highlights: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/deliver-real-world-results-with-sap-business-ai-q4-2023-amp-q1-2024-release/ba-p/13650291 

 ============================================================================

CHAPTER TITLES  

0:00 Intro 

0:07 SAP CodeJam European Roadshow  

1:04 SAP CodeTalk with Daniel Schlachter on the CAP plugin concept 

2:16 SAP Build Developer Challenge Week 3 Announcement 

3:00 FioriConf 2024 and SAP CodeJam – SAP Fiori elements flexible programming model 

3:55 Solution Experience Live Session: Deliver Real-World Results with SAP Business AI, April 30, 2024 

Transcript

Tom: This is the SAP Developer News for the 18th of April, 2024. Live from Amsterdam. Live from
Bucharest. Live from Leverkusen, Germany. Hey there, we're halfway through this year's SAP Code Jam
European Roadshow. As the little intro there demonstrated, we've hit three cities already, we've got Paris,
France, and Madrid, Spain still to go on this trip. We've had a wonderful time interacting with all the
community members, developers out there, introducing them to ABAP cloud as well as the SAP cloud
application programming model. And this is a great time to remind you that anybody can request and offer to
host an SAP Code Jam, we'll do our best to accommodate and and make that possible. So if you'd like to
see next time's road show come to an area near you, consider hosting a code jam in the future.

DJ: Recently, there's a new plugin concept that's been introduced in the SAP Cloud Application
programming model. On the one hand, it allows the CAP team to build out new functions and features for
CAP in a really beautifully modular way and it allows us as developers to consume those extensions and
new features also in a really beautifully simple way, often only requiring one line in our projects. It also allows
us in the community to contribute extensions to CAP as well. This is the CAP plugin concept and recently I
got the opportunity to talk about the CAP plugin concept and learn more about it from one of the enablement
team members Daniel Schlachter. It's in a code talk format so you can consume it in video form or in audio
form in the code talk podcast. Check it out. The link is in the description.

Shrini: Hello, SAP Builders. We are already in week three of the developer challenge. And this time, we will
be diving into the theming capabilities of SAP Build. Our very own developer advocate, Rekha, has prepared
an exciting challenge that will walk you through all the different options available in the theme, style, and
layout tabs of SAP Build apps. She has also provided step-by-step instructions on how to complete the
challenge in a post. Participate in the challenge and share your screenshots, along with the hashed answers
in the reply section of this discussion in the SAP Builders group. We are eager to see your responses to this
challenge.

Nico: Hi everyone, and welcome to the SAP Developer News. This is just a friendly reminder that FioriConf
2024 happens on April 24th. It's free, live, online only, with a 100% focus on SAP Fiori. The event is
organized by SAP community members and is a great fit for everyone working with SAP Fiori, be it
developers, consultants, architects, or admins. Make sure to check out the link in the description. And
speaking of SAP Fiori, we just added a new topic to the list of available topics for SAP Code Jam events, the
SAP Fiori Elements Flexible Programming Model. Make sure to check out the link in the description if you
want to host or attend an SAP Code Jam event, and also check out the repository with the content. Hope to
see you soon. Bye.

Nora: Many partners and customers always ask me what SAP means by business AI, and what SAP's
approach is to business AI, and how SAP can help them transform their businesses and leverage AI for their
businesses. If you also have those questions, Then listen up because SAP Learning is hosting a 16-minute
solution experience live session on the 30th of April at 1.30 Central European summertime to answer exactly
those questions. The session is suitable for all audiences as it is a get started session. And you will not only
learn about SAP's latest innovative use cases of AI, but also understand SAP's strategy towards business AI
and how your business can benefit from it. The speaker is no other than Dr. Philipp Herzig, our Chief AI
Officer, who will also guide you through the 2024 Q1 releases of Business AI and he will share all the know how with you that you need to stay up to date and to transform your business. So make sure you register
with the link below and sign up for the event and don't miss out on all the insights

entry.title: Re: How to create new data or delete, update with S/4HANA data in Build Apps?
entry.content: I'll be sure to keep an eye on this thread. Looking for the same issue. Bumped into your thread. Thanks for creating it. Looking forward for solution. Panorama Charter

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere DBADMIN and Data provisioning agent is...
entry.content:
Hi, Check out this step by step video on SAP Datasphere and DP agent configuration https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=lMug4dQeecE&ab_channel=MurlliMaraati

Hope this will help.

Best Regards,

Murlli Maraati

entry.title: The project system should automatically update the equipment usage counter
entry.content:
My requirement is to update my equipment counter via PS network.
The confirmation should be the trigger for creating the measurement document.

My Equipment is a PRT and the Counter is Type V = Usage Counter. 

For Production Order it wokred perfectly fine, but now we need to create the mesaurment dosument via the PS System. 

Is this a standard SAP functionality?

#PRT #Usage Counter #Equipment #Project System

entry.title: Re: WORKFLOW FORWARDING FROM MIGRATION USER
entry.content:
Hi 

If your workflow are based on rules then you you can reexecute the rule the determinations will then be recalculated and the work items will be affected to the right users

Otherwise you could make a report to find the recipient of a work item (function module sap_wapi_workitem_recipients) 

And then assign it using function module SAP_WAPI_ADM_WORKITEM_FORWARD

Cheers

entry.title: FPSCHEDULER future target run date is required
entry.content:
Hello Team,

We use transaction FPSCHEDULER to schedule payment runs. Our requirement is to set the target run date, one day into the future. How can we implement this requirement?

Best regards!


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: FPSCHEDULER future target run date is required
entry.content:
Hello,

to adjust the target date (and target id), the FI-CA event 1906 can be used to influence the result.

Best regards,
Ronny

entry.title: Re: Replicate requirements from MD04 for multiple materials
entry.content: Is it possible to break these out by month? Also what is the table for this data?
